---
title: "Notebooks"
---

The notebooks are student-facing companions to the slide decks. They are designed to be run, modified, and rerun.

Use the Google Colab links if you want to work in a browser without installing Python locally.

The [preparation rhythm](../preparation.qmd) explains when to use notebooks in relation to the book, slides, weekly practice, quizzes, and project work.

## How To Use Them

For each notebook:

1. run the setup cells first;
2. run the demonstration cells without changing anything;
3. change the marked parameters or `TODO` cells;
4. compare the image, residual, coefficient, or curve before and after the change;
5. answer the final coherence check in your own words.

The notebooks are not only code examples. They are experiments for the course identity:

$$
\text{measurement}
\quad+\quad
\text{prior or learned assumption}
\quad+\quad
\text{algorithm}
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
\text{reconstruction}.
$$

When a figure improves, ask what caused the improvement: stronger data consistency, a better prior, a different parameter, or a more stable algorithm.

| Week | Topic | Notebook | Google Colab |
|---:|---|---|---|
| 1 | Image formation as arrays | [Download](week01_image_formation.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week01_image_formation.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week01_image_formation.ipynb) |
| 2 | Convolution and blur | [Download](week02_convolution_blur.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week02_convolution_blur.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week02_convolution_blur.ipynb) |
| 3 | Fourier transform in imaging | [Download](week03_fourier_imaging.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week03_fourier_imaging.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week03_fourier_imaging.ipynb) |
| 4 | Noise models and likelihoods | [Download](week04_noise_likelihood.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week04_noise_likelihood.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week04_noise_likelihood.ipynb) |
| 5 | Ill-posed inverse problems | [Download](week05_ill_posed_inverse_problems.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week05_ill_posed_inverse_problems.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week05_ill_posed_inverse_problems.ipynb) |
| 6 | Tikhonov regularization | [Download](week06_tikhonov_regularization.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week06_tikhonov_regularization.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week06_tikhonov_regularization.ipynb) |
| 7 | Variational formulation | [Download](week07_variational_formulation.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week07_variational_formulation.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week07_variational_formulation.ipynb) |
| 8 | Total Variation regularization | [Download](week08_total_variation.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week08_total_variation.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week08_total_variation.ipynb) |
| 9 | Optimization methods | [Download](week09_optimization_methods.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week09_optimization_methods.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week09_optimization_methods.ipynb) |
| 10 | Sparse reconstruction | [Download](week10_sparse_reconstruction.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week10_sparse_reconstruction.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week10_sparse_reconstruction.ipynb) |
| 11 | Wavelets and multiscale representation | [Download](week11_wavelets.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week11_wavelets.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week11_wavelets.ipynb) |
| 12 | Model-based vs data-driven imaging | [Download](week12_model_data.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week12_model_data.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week12_model_data.ipynb) |
| 13 | Plug-and-play and learned regularization | [Download](week13_plug_and_play.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week13_plug_and_play.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week13_plug_and_play.ipynb) |
| 14 | Stability, robustness, and ethics | [Download](week14_stability_robustness.ipynb) / [GitHub](https://github.com/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week14_stability_robustness.ipynb) | [Open in Colab](https://colab.research.google.com/github/lnajman/math435-mathematical-imaging/blob/main/notebooks/week14_stability_robustness.ipynb) |

## Repository Examples

The slide decks use short code-reference slides so that command-line details do not interrupt the live class path. If you work locally, example scripts live in the `examples/` directory.

Typical local commands are:

```bash
python3 examples/weekXX_topic_name.py
python3 scripts/build_notebooks.py
./scripts/quarto render
```

Replace `weekXX_topic_name.py` with the example script for the week you are studying. Students who do not want a local setup should use the Colab links above.

## Working Locally

From the repository root:

```bash
python3 -m pip install -r requirements.txt
jupyter notebook
```

The notebooks use Plotly for interactive figures, so they are especially useful for changing parameters and immediately seeing the effect.
